name: Flaky Freezer Biscuits
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine yeast and sugar with warm water. Set aside for 10 minutes.
2. In a large bowl, mix flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
3. Cut in butter to form a crumbly mixture. Add the yeast mixture and buttermilk. Mix just enough to hold dough together.
4. Roll dough about 3/4 inch thick on a floured surface. Cut out biscuits with the top of a glass or cutter. Prick tops with a fork.
5. Freeze biscuits separately on a cookie sheet. Once frozen, stack and wrap well.
6. Before baking, allow dough to rise until doubled in size.
7. Bake at 425°F for 15 minutes on a lightly greased cookie sheet.
8. Makes 3-4 dozen. Grated Cheddar cheese may be added to the soft dough for flaky cheese biscuits.
